videos.title: Stop Claude from forgetting anything
videos.summary: Hermes is a memory management tool for Claude that persists context and learnings across project sessions. It maintains a unified memory store and skill library so users do not lose context when starting new projects, addressing Claude's session reset limitation.

videos.title: Hermes Agent Tutorial: How to Setup & Use Open Source AI Agent for Beginners
videos.summary: Hermes Agent is an open-source AI agent framework available on OpenRouter with nearly 200K GitHub stars. It can be set up locally on Mac or Windows for free, supports integration with Telegram, features persistent memory and cron job scheduling, and can self-improve by writing its own new skills without user intervention.

videos.title: Matt Pocock’s Agentic Engineering Workflow (just copy him)
videos.summary: David Ondrej reviews Matt Pocock's agentic engineering workflow in a YouTube video. The content focuses on practical approaches to AI-assisted coding and is sponsored by SerpApi.

videos.title: Gemma Playground: AI Edge Gallery
videos.summary: Ian Ballantyne demonstrates the Google AI Edge Gallery app running Gemma on a Pixel 10 Pro for on-device tasks including image understanding, audio transcription, agent-based app routing, and offline object identification. The app enables local inference without requiring network connectivity.

videos.title: 3 patterns to build long-running AI agents
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech discusses three core architectural patterns for long-running AI agents: event-driven dormancy, durable checkpointing, and separated evaluation. These patterns enable agents to manage multi-day workflows beyond stateless chatbots.

videos.title: Artifacts in Claude Code: share your work as it happens
videos.summary: Artifacts in Claude Code transforms raw output like data, mockups, and JSON into visual pages that teams can share and understand at a glance. The feature is available in beta on Team and Enterprise plans.

videos.title: Antigravity SDK: Building a digital simulated world
videos.summary: Google's Antigravity SDK enables creation of multi-agent social simulations with autonomous virtual avatars. A demonstration at Google I/O showed avatars of attendees interacting autonomously within a simulated space station environment.

videos.title: Enterprise-managed auth for MCP connectors
videos.summary: Anthropic has added enterprise-managed authentication for MCP connectors, allowing administrators to authorize connectors across their organization through identity providers without requiring individual user setup. This simplifies deployment and security management for enterprise users of Claude.

videos.title: 3 takeaways on Software Ecology™️
videos.summary: Adam Bender from Google for Developers discusses the implications of easy AI code generation on software systems ecology. The talk explores three key takeaways about how accessible AI-driven code generation is shaping the future of software architecture and practices.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code presents a ten-minute tutorial on Bigram Language Models, drawing from Andrej Karpathy's explanation. The video covers tokenization, embedding, vocabulary, loss functions, cross-entropy, softmax, and optimization as foundational concepts for understanding how language models process tokens and prepare for attention mechanisms and GPT.

videos.title: 7 tips for using Antigravity 2.0 on enterprise codebases, coding phase
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates seven practical tactics for deploying Antigravity 2.0, an AI agent, on large enterprise codebases. The tactics cover agent skill encoding, automated testing loops, headless browser verification, and asynchronous task offloading to improve code quality and developer productivity.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Is Missing Half the Internet… Until Now (Agent-Reach)
videos.summary: Agent-Reach is an open source tool that gives AI agents access to real internet context across Western platforms like Twitter/X, Reddit, YouTube, and GitHub as well as Chinese platforms like Bilibili and Xiaohongshu. It solves the problem of coding agents breaking when they need social feedback, discussions, tutorials, or non-English research from the internet.

videos.title: After spent 30+ hrs building loops...
videos.summary: AI Jason released a loop engineer template and knowledge base on GitHub for building AI agent loops. The resource includes a free course, step-by-step workshop, and associated tools like Superdesign for practical implementation.

videos.title: How to build an AI Agent and MCP Server (step-by-step)
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates how to build AI agents using Model Context Protocol (MCP), an open standard that enables AI agents to connect securely with live data and external tools. The video walks through connecting Google Trends with a blog writing AI agent using Google's Agent Development Kit.

videos.title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale — Sandipan Bhaumik, Databricks
videos.summary: Sandipan Bhaumik presents five pillars for production AI systems: evaluation (define success numerically), observability (trace every agent decision), data foundation (handle data quality), multi-agent orchestration, and governance (catch PII breaches). The framework emerged from a retail bank engagement where six weeks of infrastructure setup preceded model selection, and tracing detected stale knowledge after a policy update caused customer satisfaction to drop.

videos.title: Build a Real-World Multi-Agent AI App with MCP + LangGraph | Part 2
videos.summary: A tutorial on building a multi-agent travel planning system by integrating LangGraph agents with MCP servers for real-time flight and weather data. The tutorial covers connecting MCP servers, building custom MCP servers, incorporating real-time APIs, and deploying with PostgreSQL memory and Streamlit UI.

videos.title: Building AI Agents for Real-World Problems & Workflows
videos.summary: Shailaja Patel-Pranav discusses how AI agents operate in real-world systems, emphasizing the differences between research and production environments. The video covers agent orchestration, task coordination, policy enforcement, and alignment with human decision-making in workflow automation contexts.

videos.title: FULLY FREE GLM-5.2 + Z-Code: This is ACTUALLY GOOD!
videos.summary: Z Code is a Codex-like coding agent built for GLM models with a free tier offering 5 million tokens daily. It supports code generation, project creation, live previews, and DevTools integration, though file explorer and git initialization features are not yet available.

videos.title: 6 INSANE Claude Cowork Use Cases to Save Hours (AI Workflow Automation)
videos.summary: YouTube tutorial demonstrating six Claude Cowork workflows for automation use cases including morning briefing, content generation, inbox triage, research reports, interactive dashboards, and mobile agent control. The video showcases practical applications of agentic AI for reducing manual work.

videos.title: When you realize that you’re the senior dev now.
videos.summary: A YouTube video from Google for Developers featuring Pete King with humorous commentary on reaching senior developer status. The video appears to be motivational content about career advancement in software development.

videos.title: How to build a custom vision agent
videos.summary: Google Developer Expert John Capobianco demonstrates a custom vision agent that captures live webcam feeds using Nano Banana Pro and animates them with Veo 3, processing natural language prompts and ASL conversations through the Gemini ecosystem and Model Context Protocol.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: AI engineers use loops that automatically prompt agents rather than manual prompting. The technique involves an orchestrator agent spawning worker agents in parallel to complete tasks, with observability and cost control via dashboards. This compounds both capability and hallucination risk.

videos.title: China’s New AI Is 6X More Efficient Than Claude
videos.summary: Moonshot AI released Kimi K2.7 Code, an open-weight model designed for coding agents that work with real codebases. The model is positioned as an efficient alternative to closed-source coding systems.

videos.title: Every Level of Hermes Agent Explained
videos.summary: Jack Roberts explains a seven-level progression model for building Hermes agents, from simple prompting to autonomous operating systems that manage multi-agent workflows. The tutorial covers integration of Claude, ChatGPT, DeepSeek, and Grok through a unified agentic platform with skills, orchestration, and scheduled task execution.

videos.title: Converting SQL Server types and rules to PostgreSQL
videos.summary: Google Cloud demonstrates using Gemini to convert SQL Server database schemas and code to PostgreSQL equivalents. Gemini serves as an AI tutor that not only translates code constructs like user-defined types to PostgreSQL DOMAINs but also explains the conversion process to help developers build PostgreSQL expertise.

videos.title: Microsoft's launched OpenClaw?
videos.summary: Microsoft Scout is an AI agent integrated into the Microsoft ecosystem that proactively reads Teams, email, and calendar data to prepare meeting materials and handle tasks before users initiate requests. It operates as an autonomous assistant requiring minimal user direction.

videos.title: Coding with ADK to beat a text adventure game
videos.summary: A Google Cloud Tech livestream featuring Tilde Thurium and Abdel Sghiouar demonstrating the Agent Development Kit by using it to solve a text adventure game through coding and agentic AI techniques.

videos.title: AI Fact or Fiction: The Fable Ban, Tokenmaxxing, Saaspocolypse — With Ara Kharazian
videos.summary: Ara Kharazian, lead economist at Ramp, discusses whether AI spending by companies is producing real value and whether popular narratives like "tokenmaxing" and the "SaaS apocalypse" are overstated. The conversation covers Anthropic overtaking OpenAI in business adoption, DeepSeek's resurgence, Google's AI position, and whether AI investments are delivering returns.

videos.title: I Tried the Open Source ElevenLabs Alternative (Voicebox)
videos.summary: Voicebox is a free open-source local AI voice studio for developers featuring voice cloning, text-to-speech, system-wide dictation, agent voice output, and REST API. The video reviews Voicebox as a privacy-focused, local-first alternative to cloud services like ElevenLabs with unlimited generation.

videos.title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
videos.summary: AI agents often claim to have searched the web but instead fall back to stale training data when blocked by anti-bot systems, CAPTCHAs, or IP restrictions. Real web access through tools like Bright Data's Web MCP enables reliable citations, real-time results, and reduces hallucinations compared to simulated or degraded access.

videos.title: How to Build a Business Using AI: From Idea to Launch in One Day
videos.summary: A video demonstrating Rocket 1.0's ability to maintain context across AI workflows for rapid business ideation and MVP creation. The video tests whether Rocket solves the problem of losing context when switching between tools like Perplexity, Claude, Cursor, and spreadsheets.

videos.title: AI agents can manage your passwords. Should we let them? Plus: The biggest Patch Tuesday ever.
videos.summary: IBM Security Intelligence podcast explores whether AI agents should be trusted to detect compromised passwords and change them automatically, discussing the tension between cybersecurity hygiene challenges and agent readiness. The episode also covers Microsoft's June 2026 Patch Tuesday release addressing over 200 CVEs and executives' increasing willingness to accept cyber risk for innovation.

videos.title: Minimax Code + M3 : Crazy Alternative to Claude,Codex!
videos.summary: MiniMax M3 is an open-weights AI model focused on coding agents and long-context workflows, supporting up to 1 million token context windows. It is compatible with both Anthropic and OpenAI API endpoints and natively supports multimodal inputs including images and video.

videos.title: POV: A new feature enters the chat. 🕺✨
videos.summary: Google for Developers posted a humorous video expressing sympathy for software engineers dealing with new feature rollouts. The piece uses a lighthearted "POV" format to comment on the challenges developers face when new functionality enters their workflow.

videos.title: New #1 open-source AI model is here!
videos.summary: GLM 5.2 is presented as a leading open-source AI model capable of multimodal tasks including digital earth rendering, animation with voiceover, 3D model generation, ray tracing, music composition, and mathematics visualization. The model is accessible via web chat, can integrate with Claude, and offers a coding plan subscription.

videos.title: Fable 5 Replacement Just Dropped: Fusion (Fable Level AI)
videos.summary: OpenRouter introduced Fusion, a system that routes a single prompt to multiple AI models, compares their outputs, identifies knowledge gaps, and synthesizes them into a unified response. A budget Fusion configuration achieved performance close to Fable 5 on deep research benchmarks at roughly half the cost.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS provides AI coding agents with up-to-date documentation, best practices, and tested procedures for working with AWS services. It replaces reliance on agents' general knowledge with specific guardrails and resources that improve success rates and reduce wasted effort.

videos.title: The Briefing: Financial Services
videos.summary: Anthropic hosted a virtual briefing on Claude deployment in financial services, featuring leaders from major banks sharing implementations. The event showcased organizational adoption of Claude across the banking sector.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 with Harness Engineering & Loops Will Do Everything.
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof covers Claude Fable 5 and discusses harness engineering, AI loops, and their applications in regulated industries. The video explores tensions between open and closed source AI models, risks of model shutdowns, and the state of AI-powered SaaS, drawing on experience since 2023 in the space.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS Developers walkthrough shows how to run Claude Code, Anthropic's agentic coding tool, on Amazon Bedrock. The video covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, cost tracking per team, and content governance via Bedrock Guardrails to keep data within AWS infrastructure.

videos.title: Building long-running AI agents with ADK
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech presents a tutorial on building long-running AI agents using the open source Agent Development Kit (ADK). The tutorial demonstrates the long-running agent pattern for stateless, event-driven systems with multi-agent delegation where a coordinator agent delegates specialized tasks to sub-agents.

videos.title: Better Free AI Image than Midjourney?
videos.summary: Jack Roberts argues that Ideogram 4.0 solves the longstanding problem of text rendering in AI image generation, outperforming Midjourney and Google's Nano Banana Pro on certain benchmarks. He positions it as a free alternative suitable for brand asset generation.

videos.title: Machiavelli is the most misunderstood thinker of all time – Ada Palmer
videos.summary: Ada Palmer discusses Machiavelli's life and political philosophy on the Dwarkesh Patel podcast. She argues that Machiavelli, a Florentine diplomat tortured and exiled by the Medici, compiled his observations on power and human nature into a book he kept restricted because he feared misuse of his insights could threaten Florence. Palmer draws a parallel to Szilard's nuclear warning to FDR.

videos.title: Building with ADK & A2A: How multi-agent collaboration designs a game
videos.summary: Google Cloud demonstrates multi-agent collaboration using Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Agent2Agent protocol (A2A) to design and build a game. The demo shows distinct AI agents working together on architecture, rendering, and project management within a human-in-the-loop framework.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that while a ChatGPT-like moment for robotics is approaching, the industry's focus on humanoid form factors driven by companies like 1X and Unitree is myopic. She contends that designing robots for human delight and interaction, rather than utility alone, will be critical as billions of robots enter society.

videos.title: Is Unstructured Data The Key To Successful AI Deployments?
videos.summary: A panel discussion featuring Hyland executives and an insurance CIO argues that unstructured data is critical for successful AI deployments and agentic enterprises. The discussion covers how enterprises can use unstructured data to improve AI pilots and provide agents with necessary context for business impact.

videos.title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
videos.summary: Ziv Ilan's team at NVIDIA reduced diffusion denoising steps from fifty to as few as one by combining dynamic quantization, latent caching, and step distillation. The approach stacks multiple optimization layers that can be applied independently or together to achieve 10x to 200x speedup for real-time video generation on Blackwell hardware.

videos.title: Gemini Omni Tutorial: How to Use Gemini Omni AI for Beginners
videos.summary: YouTube tutorial demonstrating Google Gemini Omni's capabilities for AI video editing and generation. The presenter tests physics simulation, character consistency, conversational editing workflows, and real footage manipulation, comparing Omni Flash against competitors like Suno Dance 2 and identifying current limitations.

videos.title: Kagenti’s Approach to Multi-Agent Security for AI Agents
videos.summary: Kagenti proposes identity-based security for multi-agent AI systems to prevent data leaks and breaches in production. The approach uses identity management, delegation chains, and open source infrastructure to secure communication between agents.

videos.title: This command SAVED my broken AI coding session
videos.summary: A YouTube video from JavaScript Mastery describes a command that resolved issues in an AI-assisted coding workflow. The specific command and its mechanics are detailed in the full video.

videos.title: Tencent Workbuddy: RIP Claude,Codex! This is ACTUALLY quite a bit CRAZY!
videos.summary: Tencent WorkBuddy is a desktop AI productivity agent that handles file analysis, spreadsheet work, report generation, and presentations. It includes 100+ domain-specific expert agents, team collaboration, remote task control via Slack/Telegram/Discord, and integrations with GitHub, Jira, Google Drive, and Notion.

videos.title: Run Gemma on the edge with the Coral Board
videos.summary: Google's Coral Board is a low-power development board with an integrated Coral NPU accelerator for running machine learning models on edge devices. It runs Gemma locally and comes with I/O peripherals including a screen, camera, microphones, and LEDs to enable on-device AI applications like live translation and multimodal inference.

videos.title: Google Just Revealed What Comes After AGI And It’s Shocking
videos.summary: A YouTube video discussing Google DeepMind's "From AGI to ASI" paper, which argues that AGI may be a transition point rather than an endpoint. The video suggests that once human-level AI is achieved, its replication, acceleration, and use in agent teams could catalyze a jump to artificial superintelligence (ASI) with potentially greater impact than AGI itself.

videos.title: Stop testing your SAAS manually
videos.summary: KaneAI is an AI-powered testing tool that automates QA by accepting plain-English test descriptions, generating and running tests automatically, and reporting results. It reduces manual testing effort and costs significantly for SaaS deployments.

videos.title: This MCP makes Hermes Agent 10x more powerful
videos.summary: Apify MCP Connectors integrate with Hermes Agent to significantly enhance agent capabilities. The video demonstrates how this integration makes the agent approximately 10 times more powerful and provides resources for implementation.

videos.title: How to build reliable software with AI agents
videos.summary: A discussion on building reliable software with AI agents, emphasizing that human-in-the-loop verification and rigorous code review are essential to ensure coding agents reduce maintenance costs rather than increase technical debt when used at scale.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Banned... Do THIS Right Now
videos.summary: Jack Roberts discusses Claude Fable 5 being delisted and argues that users should deploy models locally to avoid vendor lock-in. He demonstrates running models via Ollama and building a NotebookLM alternative with open notebook software, advocating for local hosting as a resilience strategy.

videos.title: SpaceX’s IPO Triumph, Anthropic’s Fable Fumble, OpenAI’s Price War
videos.summary: Alex Kantrowitz and Ranjan Roy discuss SpaceX's IPO success and its implications for AI competitors like OpenAI and Anthropic. The episode covers Anthropic's botched Fable product launch, speculation about pricing strategy, potential commodity pricing of AI models, and OpenAI's rumored price cuts.

videos.title: The magic behind GenLatte: Flutter, Firebase, and Gemini
videos.summary: GenLatte is a demo application showcased at Google Cloud Next '26 that uses Flutter, Firebase, and Gemini to generate dynamic user interface elements. The app leverages agentic features and Agent-to-UI (A2UI) to create personalized latte art images in real time based on individual customer preferences.

videos.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
videos.summary: Omnigent is a meta-harness that enables orchestration of multiple coding agent models such as Claude Code, Codex, and Pi as interchangeable workers. It includes features for task planning, implementation routing, code review coordination, human-in-the-loop governance, and cross-device session management.

videos.title: Why MCP and ChatGPT Apps Use Double Iframes — Frédéric Barthelet, Alpic
videos.summary: Double iframe nesting is used by MCP and ChatGPT apps to balance security and functionality. The outer iframe loads a lightweight script from a controlled subdomain, which then loads the actual app HTML via srcdoc into an inner frame, preventing CSP violations, storage access collisions, and sandbox escapes.

videos.title: In production, which file actually gets loaded?
videos.summary: Google for Developers poses a question about which package file gets loaded in production despite identical application code and package names. The clip highlights how deployment can cause different behavior due to file resolution during imports.

videos.title: What Is AI Pair Programming? AI Coding Tools for Developers
videos.summary: IBM Technology presents an overview of AI pair programming and how AI coding tools assist developers. The content highlights AI's role in improving debugging, code review, and developer productivity through collaborative workflows with AI systems.

videos.title: FULLY FREE Unlimited API + OpenCode: MiniMax M3,Step 3.7 Flash,Nemotron 3 Ultra,GLM,Kimi!
videos.summary: OpenRouter has launched Fusion API, a compound model system that combines responses from multiple models using a judge model to produce final answers. The system claims Fable-level intelligence at lower cost but real-world testing shows inconsistent performance relative to using single strong models directly.

videos.title: Why I never trust fully generated Code
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery author expresses distrust in fully generated code from AI systems. The piece represents a viewpoint on code generation reliability and best practices for developers.

videos.title: The Internet Is No Longer Human
videos.summary: A video essay arguing that bots and AI agents now dominate internet traffic and behavior, reducing human engagement through AI Overviews and autonomous shopping agents. The piece warns that the web's human-centric model is shifting toward machine-driven interaction.

videos.title: STOP Using Claude Code Without This Fable 5 Agentic OS
videos.summary: Chase AI presents a video tutorial on building a custom Claude Code Agentic OS called Fable 5, covering architecture setup, memory systems, and observability. The framework enables developers to create autonomous agents using Claude Code with enhanced efficiency through modular skills and customization.

videos.title: You Don't Need React Portals For Modals Anymore (react-call)
videos.summary: react-call is a lightweight React utility that allows calling components as asynchronous functions. It uses Root/Stack architecture, upsert singletons, and mutation flow patterns to simplify modal management and eliminate state tracking boilerplate compared to React Portals.

videos.title: Type This Into Claude, It'll Make You Build 10x Faster.
videos.summary: Austin Marchese presents six "power phrases" to use with Claude Code for faster development, claiming they improve project initiation, scaling, and autonomous execution. He positions these as techniques endorsed by Claude Code's creator and Andrej Karpathy, distinct from better prompts or smarter models.

videos.title: Smart AI routing explained
videos.summary: IBM Technology presents a shift in AI lab focus away from building the largest models toward implementing smart routing mechanisms. The commentary highlights cost efficiency and trust as key drivers for this strategic change.

videos.title: How to ACTUALLY Use AI For Coding
videos.summary: OpenAI's Codex introduces a /goal command that automates multi-step code generation through a loop called Ralph. Users describe the desired outcome in plain English and the tool plans, executes, tests, and self-corrects without further user input.

videos.title: Bridging Design Systems and Code with MCP and AI Agents
videos.summary: Design systems and context engineering are used to guide AI agents in building consistent, correct applications. MCP and AI agents work together to bridge design systems and code generation.

videos.title: Build a Hermes Knowledge Base That Self-Improves
videos.summary: A tutorial on building a self-improving knowledge base for the Hermes AI agent using Obsidian as a wiki vault connected to Claude and NotebookLM. The workflow integrates meeting notes from Granola and research via automatic cron jobs to create persistent, indexed context that improves agent performance over time.

videos.title: OpenRouter Fusion: They OFFICIALLY CLAIM THAT THIS MODEL beats FABLE?
videos.summary: OpenRouter launched Fusion API, a compound model that routes prompts to multiple models in parallel and uses a judge model to synthesize answers. The vendor claims Fable-level performance at lower cost, but real-world testing shows inconsistent results in coding, math, and generation tasks, often underperforming single models.

videos.title: RIP Claude Fable, open-source AI unleashed, full body avatars, new Google models, new TTS: AI NEWS
videos.summary: AI Search YouTube channel presents a news roundup covering GLM 5.2, Kimi K2.7, Claude Fable deprecation, and releases including Minimax M3, DiffusionGemma, Luma Agents, Dots TTS, and numerous research projects on video, 3D avatars, and language models. The episode highlights emerging capabilities in multimodal translation, diffusion models, and agent systems.

videos.title: 3,000+ Tokens Per Second? Let's Stress Test Xiaomi's New Model: MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed
videos.summary: Xiaomi and TileRT released a 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model capable of 1,000+ tokens per second on standard hardware. The architecture uses selective FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a persistent GPU kernel runtime to achieve this throughput performance.

videos.title: Fable 5 is never coming back, here's why
videos.summary: David Ondrej reports that Fable 5 has been banned and frames this as the beginning of a permanent underclass. The video discusses implications of the ban without technical details about the tool itself.

videos.title: This Is Bad... They Just Shut Down FABLE 5
videos.summary: Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models were shut down by US government order after only three days of public availability. The video raises concerns about whether frontier AI systems will remain accessible to the public or be restricted to government control.

videos.title: GPT-6 Is Launching Into a World OpenAI No Longer Controls
videos.summary: A YouTube video analyzing GPT-6 leaks and rumors, arguing that OpenAI has lost competitive ground while Microsoft, Apple, and Google have integrated alternative models. Coverage includes Microsoft's Project Polaris in GitHub Copilot, Apple's $1B/year Gemini deal, Claude Fable 5 topping benchmarks, memory advances in Dreaming V3, and OpenAI's planned IPO alongside business losses.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Cole Medin discusses the sudden withdrawal of Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 model following a US government export-control directive related to alleged jailbreak vulnerabilities. The video examines the regulatory action, its overnight effect across all customers, and implications for developers building on large language models.

videos.title: 1,000+ Tokens/Sec: Google Just Shattered the AI Speed Limit (DiffusionGemma)
videos.summary: Google DeepMind released DiffusionGemma, a model that applies diffusion-based generation techniques to text generation using uniform state diffusion to iteratively refine predictions over bidirectional passes. The architecture shifts inference from memory-bound to compute-bound, achieving generation speeds exceeding 1,000 tokens per second on an H100 GPU while trading off some output quality.

videos.title: This AI Chinese model is 15X cheaper?
videos.summary: MiniMax M3 is a long-context AI model released on OpenRouter that uses Sparse Attention to process documents 15.6x faster and at one-twentieth the compute cost of prior MiniMax generations. The model efficiently prioritizes relevant input while ignoring irrelevant content.

videos.title: GLM-5.2 (Fully Tested): I got EARLY ACCESS & This MODEL is CRAZY!
videos.summary: GLM-5.2 is an improved coding model from Z AI featuring a 1 million token context window and expected open-weight release under MIT license. It achieves performance around 81.43 on coding benchmarks, roughly 6 percent below Claude 3.5 Opus, and costs approximately 8 dollars per month through the GLM Coding Plan.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
videos.summary: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

videos.title: BREAKING: US Government BANNED Mythos and Fable
videos.summary: A YouTube video claims the US government issued an export control directive requiring Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models for all customers immediately. The report references a purported Anthropic news announcement on the action.

videos.title: Google’s New AI Just Broke The AI Speed Limit: DiffusionGemma
videos.summary: Google released DiffusionGemma, an open AI model that generates text via a different mechanism and runs up to four times faster on dedicated GPUs. The model features a 256-token canvas and built-in self-correction capabilities.

videos.title: Inspect and live-edit 3D WebGL elements
videos.summary: Google's HTML-in-Canvas API enables developers to keep UI elements within 3D WebGL environments fully accessible, searchable, and inspectable using standard developer tools. The API addresses the challenge of maintaining accessibility and debuggability in WebGL-based applications.

videos.title: Which Antigravity surface should I use? 2.0 vs CLI vs IDE vs SDK
videos.summary: Google Antigravity is an agent-first software development platform that enables developers to build, orchestrate, and monitor AI agents. It provides multiple interfaces including a desktop app, IDE, CLI, and SDK to support different agentic workflows.

videos.title: AIventure: Vibe Coding Journey
videos.summary: AIventure is an open source retro dungeon crawler built on Angular and Phaser.js that teaches vibe coding and agentic behaviors using Gemma 4. The game demonstrates prompting NPCs to generate web apps and autonomous reasoning loops, with model serving via Transformers.js, Ollama, LM Studio, Gemini API, or Google Cloud.

videos.title: If you don’t run Pi locally you’re falling behind…
videos.summary: David Ondrej argues that running Pi locally is essential to avoid falling behind in AI development. The video discusses Pi agents as a key technology for building coding agents and emphasizes the importance of hands-on local implementation.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Just Changed Website Design Forever
videos.summary: Jack Roberts compares Claude Fable 5 against Opus 4.8 for website design generation across three difficulty levels. He argues Fable 5 excels at extracting design patterns and building from visual blueprints, with success hinging on token efficiency and prompt strategy.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is smarter💡
videos.summary: IBM Technology covers Claude Fable 5 model upgrades including better planning, deeper code analysis, and improved speed. The discussion appears in the Mixture of Experts episode series.

videos.title: Claude FM 🎵 music for thinking and building
videos.summary: Claude FM is a curated music service designed to support focused thinking and building activities. The playlist is made and curated by musicians for this specific purpose.

videos.title: Claude FM 🎵 music for thinking and building
videos.summary: Claude FM is a music curation and streaming service designed to accompany focused work on thinking and building. The music is made and curated by musicians specifically for productivity.

videos.title: How to Use Higgsfield AI Like a PRO – AI Marketing Workflow
videos.summary: Higgsfield Supercomputer is an AI super-agent that automates multi-format content creation, transforming a single prompt into a full marketing campaign including TV ads, podcast clips, vertical reels, and character-driven shorts. The system maintains character and style consistency across scenes and integrates multiple workflows like cinematic-flow, video-adapt, and podcast-flow.

videos.title: Converting SQL Server code to PostgreSQL
videos.summary: Google Cloud demonstrates using Gemini as an AI tutor to convert SQL Server T-SQL code to PostgreSQL. The tutorial shows translating data types like IDENTITY and BIT while explaining the conversion process to help developers learn PostgreSQL.

videos.title: The Rise of Generative UI for Developers (CopilotKit)
videos.summary: CopilotKit is a framework for integrating AI agents with frontend applications, enabling generative UI where agents render real components. It supports shared state management, human-in-the-loop approval flows, and agent-native interfaces beyond bolted-on chatbots, positioning itself as an alternative to Vercel AI SDK and custom implementations.

videos.title: How I make my AI Agent remember everything
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery covers techniques for enabling AI agents to retain and recall information across interactions. The content demonstrates practical implementation approaches for agent memory systems.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how to compete against much larger incumbents by building a complete enterprise platform rather than single features. She shares lessons from observing inefficient planning practices at Google and explains her hiring philosophy and trust-building strategy for acquiring early enterprise customers.

videos.title: Don’t miss this Claude update
videos.summary: Anthropic released Dynamic Workflows in Claude Opus 4.8, enabling the model to launch hundreds of subagents in parallel within a single session. The system automatically plans work, divides tasks, and creates verification workflows where subagents check each other's output.

videos.title: The $20,000 API Key Mistake
videos.summary: Keith AI presents a case study of a $20,000 incident involving improper API key handling. The video serves as a warning about the financial risks of security mismanagement in AI API usage.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 & Apple’s NVIDIA deal
videos.summary: A Mixture of Experts podcast hosted by Tim Hwang discusses whether Claude Fable 5 is overhyped, analyzing its tier routing system and Mythos-class architecture. The panel debates Apple's shift away from on-device AI toward NVIDIA cloud compute announced at WWDC 2026 and its consumer implications. Hosts also explore whether AI models can detect sarcasm without multimodal input.

videos.title: The “Final Boss” of AI Workflows: UltraCode
videos.summary: Keith AI discusses UltraCode as a solution for AI workflows, framing it as an advanced or comprehensive approach to workflow management. The video title suggests UltraCode represents a significant capability in the AI workflow space.

videos.title: Claude Code (Fable) V/S Codex (GPT-5.5) : MATHEMATICAL TEST = ONE CLEAR WINNER!
videos.summary: A video analysis comparing AI subscription plans (ChatGPT Plus, ChatGPT Pro, Claude Pro, Claude Max) to API pricing, claiming subscriptions offer substantially higher value-to-cost ratios when heavily used. The analysis suggests a $20 Claude Pro plan provides around $400 in API equivalent usage, while a $200 ChatGPT Pro plan could provide up to $14,000 in equivalent value.

videos.title: Master Claude Code with Claude Fable 5 (57 mins)
videos.summary: Keith AI's 57-minute video demonstrates advanced use of Claude Code as a coding system rather than a chat interface. The video covers Desktop, CLI, and web modes, permissions, connectors, slash commands, plugins, routines, sub-agents, and dynamic workflows for building apps and automating development workflows.

videos.title: Code with Claude Tokyo 2026: Opening Keynote
videos.summary: Anthropic held a Code with Claude 2026 opening keynote in Tokyo, Japan featuring updates from the company's engineering and product leaders. The event served as a venue for sharing latest developments and strategic directions.

videos.title: See how Gemma  can explore, plan, and scale!
videos.summary: Google demonstrates how Gemma, ADK, BigQuery MCP, and Cloud Run can be integrated to explore large datasets and optimize business operations. The video showcases practical capabilities for data exploration, planning, and scaling workflows.

videos.title: The Fable 5 Backlash Is Getting Serious
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 is facing significant backlash over overly aggressive safety guardrails that refuse harmless prompts, trigger false alarms, and limit AI-related work without transparency. The controversy centers on whether users can trust the model they receive or if it is being downgraded or shaped behind the scenes by invisible constraints.

videos.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

videos.title: Yossi Matias on the golden age of research
videos.summary: Yossi Matias, VP and Head of Google Research, discusses the "magic cycle" of research from breakthrough to real-world deployment. The conversation covers AI tools for scientific discovery including Gemini for Science, Co-Scientist, and Empirical Research Assistance, alongside advances in speculative decoding, generative UI, and quantum computing with the Willow chip.

videos.title: Your first agent configuration with MCP and Looker
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates how to build AI-powered agents using the Model Context Protocol, Agent Development Kit, and Looker. The tutorial covers MCP configuration, database tooling, securing API credentials, and creating agents that translate prompts into data queries.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 + YouTube = $30,000/mo
videos.summary: A YouTube automation system combining Claude Code Fable 5, Higsfield CLI, and Auphonic to create and edit video content at low cost. The pipeline involves reverse-engineering successful channels, writing scripts, recording voice-overs, and using Claude to automatically edit videos together into finished YouTube content.

videos.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
videos.summary: WorkOS Studio is an internal workspace that allows anyone to ask business questions against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion in natural language. The LLM generates reusable declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, making subsequent runs deterministic and cheap. Reliability is achieved through preflight sequencing with schema injection, layering rules that enforce source verification, and query validation before hardcoding into widgets.

videos.title: Running 128 Coding Agents at Once
videos.summary: Cursor's Sam Whitmore, Baseten's Charlie O'Neill and Harry Partridge discuss scaling coding agents to run in large parallel batches of 128 and propose moving toward multi-agent systems where agents message and review each other rather than isolated parallel execution.

videos.title: Meta' Secret Plan To Kill The Smartphone — With Alex Himel
videos.summary: Alex Himel, Meta's VP of Wearables, discusses Meta's vision of AI-powered smart glasses as the next computing platform. The conversation covers how AI assistants could augment daily tasks, the competitive landscape with OpenAI, Google, Apple and Amazon, privacy concerns around facial recognition, and the role of on-device AI in enabling wearable computing.

videos.title: Local AI Agents Masterclass (Gemma 4 + OpenCode + Odysseus tutorial)
videos.summary: Jordan Urbs demonstrates how to run two types of AI agents locally: Odysseus (a workspace agent) for task orchestration and OpenCode (a build agent) for code generation. The setup uses Gemma 4 via Ollama for local inference with Venice API as fallback, deployed in Docker for privacy-focused local execution.

videos.title: 1,000 AI agents, 0 LLM calls?
videos.summary: A technique for scaling AI agents by replacing LLM calls with deterministic logic and route planning algorithms. The approach uses a before_model_callback mechanism to avoid unnecessary model invocations while maintaining agent behavior, demonstrated with 1,000 agents executing with zero LLM calls.

videos.title: 21 Hermes Lessons to Get You Started
videos.summary: Jack Roberts presents 21 lessons or use cases for Hermes, a language model. The content is educational guidance on getting started with the model.

videos.title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
videos.summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard from Google that provides AI agents with structured tools and named, typed actions instead of requiring DOM scraping and pixel-level interactions. It offers both declarative APIs using HTML attributes and imperative APIs using JavaScript for complex workflows, currently in early preview on Chrome 146.

videos.title: What is MCP? Learn MCP Client, MCP Server & MCP Architecture
videos.summary: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standard enabling AI agents and applications to connect with external tools, APIs, databases, and services through a common interface. The protocol uses JSON-RPC for client-server communication and supports multiple transport mechanisms including STDIO and HTTP.

videos.title: Your Attention Is the Bottleneck, Not Your Agents — Zack Proser, WorkOS
videos.summary: Zack Proser argues that human attention, not agent speed, is the bottleneck in AI-assisted software development. He proposes a four-layer stack: signal agents that monitor Slack and Linear; verification gates using lint, build, and browser steps; weekly self-improvement loops over conversation history; and biometric feedback via Oura ring integration with Claude over MCP.

videos.title: Gemini Omni's Best Feature
videos.summary: Google's Gemini Omni can add objects to existing video footage by accepting text, image, or audio descriptions and automatically handling lighting and shading. It enables real-time post-production-style object insertion into existing clips.

videos.title: "An endless demand for compute" | Jonathan Ross, founder of Groq
videos.summary: Groq is a hardware chip specifically designed for LLM inference. Jonathan Ross, the founder, discusses Groq's LPU architecture, its comparison to GPUs via Pareto curves and cost-per-token metrics, and a strategic agreement with NVIDIA. The interview covers technical aspects like static scheduling and mixture-of-experts models.

videos.title: AI That’s Too Dangerous For You? What we learned from S.A.T.A.N
videos.summary: IBM Technology discusses how AI is accelerating the discovery of zero-day vulnerabilities and reshaping cybersecurity defense strategies. The content examines AI-driven vulnerability scanning and its implications for DevSecOps practices and system safety.

videos.title: The AI Skill I use to prevent refactors
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery discusses an AI-based skill or technique for preventing code refactors. The content suggests practical application of AI in software development workflows to improve code quality and maintainability.

videos.title: THE $1 CODER: This CODER COSTS $1 AND GIVES YOU $50 WORTH USAGE!
videos.summary: Command Code is a coding agent harness optimized for open-source models including DeepSeek, Kimi, GLM, Qwen, and MiniMax. It improves agent performance through routing, caching, context handling, and tool-call repair, with features like plan mode, IDE integration, and MCP support. The service offers affordable paid plans starting at one dollar per month with credits for testing on real coding tasks.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 series with Nano, Super, and Ultra variants. The architecture combines hybrid Mamba transformer, MTP, and Latent MoE components. The video analyzes how these architectural innovations distinguish Nemotron from other open models.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is here!
videos.summary: A YouTube video review of Claude Fable 5, demonstrating its capabilities across tasks like ray tracing, 3D reconstruction, music composition, and chemistry course generation. The video compares Fable 5 against GPT 5.5 and covers model benchmarks and specifications.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open-source command-line tool for building and deploying AI agents to production. The tool provides features including agent identity management, sandboxed code execution, audit trails, and integrates with AI coding assistants like Claude Code. It enables rapid prototyping and deployment of production-grade AI agents.

videos.title: I One-Shotted An Entire App With Claude Fable 5 (Full Breakdown)
videos.summary: Jordan Urbs demonstrates building a video editing app in one hour by feeding Claude Fable 5 only screenshots of a SaaS landing page via Claude Code. The model successfully generated functional code including filler removal, clip detection, and subtitle export, though Fable 5 consumes API limits 2x faster than Opus and will be discontinued on June 22.

videos.title: 3 rules to keeping your code healthy!
videos.summary: Google for Developers presents three rules for maintaining healthy code, emphasizing that good identifier naming is about context rather than length. The video is hosted by speaker Jasmyne Roberts.

videos.title: Build a calendar app in AI Studio in 6 minutes
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates building a no-code calendar app in Google AI Studio using Gemini to extract travel itineraries and generate calendar invitations. The tutorial showcases advanced reasoning capabilities and error troubleshooting within a visual development environment.

videos.title: Don't use Fable 5 in Claude… do this instead
videos.summary: David Ondrej recommends not using Fable 5 with Claude and suggests an alternative approach. The video discusses coding with AI and references AutoGit, a tool for AI-assisted development.

videos.title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawford, Snorkel
videos.summary: Kobie Crawford demonstrates that smaller models (4B) finetuned with RL to master tool discipline (like calling get_table_name before querying) outperform much larger models (235B) on SQL reasoning tasks. Single-table training transferred to multi-table problems, improving FinQA performance from 13.9% to 26.6%, with training costs under $500.

videos.title: The Problem Solvers | Michael Truell at Cursor
videos.summary: Cursor is an AI-native coding platform that uses agents to help engineers and less technical builders ship quality code. The company has grown from 15 people to 700 in two years and now serves over 60% of Fortune 500 companies. Cursor has partnered closely with Anthropic throughout its growth.

videos.title: Stop applying AI to the old ways of working!
videos.summary: Andrew Macvean argues that organizations should not simply apply AI to existing workflows, as coding was never the true bottleneck. He advocates for rethinking entire development lifecycles and business processes to be designed around AI capabilities rather than retrofitting AI into traditional practices.

videos.title: New AI attack reality💥
videos.summary: University of Toronto researchers created an AI worm, prompting discussion about cybersecurity implications. The content argues that stopping AI entirely is infeasible and that defensive strategies must instead focus on adaptation rather than prevention.

videos.title: PAI: The Life OS for AI-Powered Development
videos.summary: PAI (Personal AI Infrastructure) is a Life OS built on Claude Code that provides AI agents with persistent memory of project context, custom skills, and structured workflows. It uses a seven-phase algorithm to help developers maintain continuity across coding sessions and avoid re-explaining project architecture and decisions to their AI assistant.

videos.title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
videos.summary: Gemma 4's 31B model achieves fourth place on LM Arena's open model leaderboard while running on a single GPU, requiring half to a quarter the compute of comparable competitors. The model supports deployment on phones, multiple GPUs for hospital systems, and multi-agent workloads on consumer hardware via LM Studio, with an Apache 2.0 license enabling deployment in sovereign contexts without extended procurement delays.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Design – Full AI Design Workflow 2026
videos.summary: Claude Design is an AI design tool that generates branded design assets and landing pages from design systems. The tutorial demonstrates a 4-step workflow: building a design system, generating designs, editing with markups, and deploying templates. According to the creator, it replaces Figma for most design tasks.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 (TESTED): UHM... It's actually not worth it..
videos.summary: AICodeKing tests Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 models, noting strong benchmark performance on coding and reasoning tasks but finding limited real-world value improvement over existing alternatives. The author highlights Fable 5 as a safeguarded version of Mythos 5 with classifier-based restrictions, and raises concerns about remaining agent reliability issues and pricing costs.

videos.title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
videos.summary: PostHog is developing an automated pipeline that detects product signals such as rage clicks, error spikes, and customer messages, then uses a background agent to group them, research code, and open pull requests autonomously. The system reduces the manual triage and development cycle by collapsing detection to fix into an automated workflow.

videos.title: AI agents explained: Build your first agent in 8 minutes
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates how to build a fully autonomous, self-correcting multi-agent system using the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK). The video covers the ReAct Framework, three main agent patterns (sequential, reactive, planning), and includes a practical example of a Blog Writing Agent that combines Planner and Writer agents with auto-correction capabilities.

videos.title: The One Mistake Everyone Makes with AI
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery published a video discussing a widespread mistake that people make when working with AI. The video is framed as educational content for developers integrating AI into applications.

videos.title: Can you social engineer an AI? Plus: AI worms and the nonhuman identity problem
videos.summary: IBM Security Intelligence podcast explores social engineering attacks against AI systems, including a Meta/Instagram prompt injection incident and a self-replicating AI worm from University of Toronto. The discussion examines whether AI agents are more susceptible to manipulation than humans and highlights security risks from unaudited nonhuman identities in organizations.

videos.title: Well, that’s a hot take on light mode for sure!
videos.summary: Google for Developers presenters Bri Davis and Justine Lai discuss light mode's effect on keeping users awake and its tradeoffs. The video frames light mode as a "hot take" subject, questioning whether the alertness benefit justifies the cost.

videos.title: Anthropic Just Dropped Fable 5 And It’s Terrifying
videos.summary: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, its first publicly available Mythos-class AI model, with capabilities in software engineering, knowledge work, vision, analytics, scientific research, and cybersecurity. The company implemented safety systems to limit its full capabilities in high-risk domains like cybersecurity, biology, and chemistry.

videos.title: $10,000 AI Website in 28mins
videos.summary: Jack Roberts demonstrates building a website comparable to a $10,000 professional project in just 28 minutes using AI tools. The video showcases rapid AI-assisted web development and the efficiency gains from automation.

videos.title: From prompt to a fully working real-time voice app in seconds. 🎙️✨
videos.summary: Google AI Studio enables developers to build real-time voice AI applications from natural language prompts. The platform automates the development, deployment, and iteration of agentic workflows directly from voice specifications.

videos.title: Apple’s New Siri AI Is Now Gemini
videos.summary: Apple revealed a rebuilt Siri AI at WWDC 2026 with personal context, screen awareness, Visual Intelligence, and cross-device integration across iPhone, iPad, Mac, Apple Watch, and Vision Pro. The new Siri is reportedly powered by Google Gemini, and regulatory delays will prevent EU launch at release. The announcement did not improve investor sentiment despite the feature upgrades.

videos.title: Claude Mythos is FINALLY here (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Anthropic has released Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos class model, which achieves top performance on benchmarks including SWE Bench Pro and Frontier Code. The model demonstrates capabilities in vision, code generation, long-context reasoning, and handles various tasks from website testing to fullstack development.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 beats Pokémon FireRed only using vision
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 completed Pokémon FireRed using only visual input from game screenshots, without maps or navigation aids. This demonstrates improved multimodal reasoning compared to earlier Claude models that required helper systems.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discuss job hunting strategies for tech professionals in 2026 as AI transforms hiring. The video covers networking, personal branding, open source contributions, and argues that junior developers remain valuable despite AI automation rather than being replaced entirely.

videos.title: 21 Hermes concepts to get you started
videos.summary: Jack Roberts presents 21 foundational concepts related to Hermes, a model or framework. The video serves as an introductory guide for learners new to the system.

videos.title: Multiple claude agent in the same window?
videos.summary: Claude Opus 4.8 introduces Dynamic Workflows, which enables launching hundreds of subagents concurrently within a single session. The system automatically plans work distribution, assigns parallel tasks, and performs verification through dedicated quality assurance agents.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

videos.title: Claude just dropped UltraCode... Unlock SuperPowers
videos.summary: Claude released UltraCode, a feature that enables code-based orchestration of up to 10 parallel agents without a master LLM. The video tutorial covers implementation patterns including judge panels, adversarial verification, deterministic fan-out, and integration with OpenRouter for dynamic model selection.

videos.title: Build an app from your phone in AI Studio
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates how to build a custom image-generation app directly from a mobile browser using Google AI Studio, voice commands, and the Nano Banana API. The tutorial shows real-time code generation without requiring software installation.

videos.title: Behind the Scenes - Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Brian Yu - Chapter 1 - Playing
videos.summary: CS50 is Harvard University's introductory course on computer science and AI, instructed by David J. Malan and Brian Yu. It is available through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, and Harvard Summer School, with accompanying community resources across various social media and collaboration platforms.

videos.title: GPU Cloud Deployment Without Leaving Your IDE — Audry Hsu, RunPod
videos.summary: Flash is a deployment tool that replaces traditional Docker-based GPU cloud workflows with a single @flash.endpoint decorator on async Python functions. It enables hot reload for model swaps and automatic deployment to GPU cloud infrastructure from the IDE.

videos.title: Sarah Paine - Why Putin and Xi can't escape geography
videos.summary: Military historian Sarah Paine presents a two-thousand-year geopolitical framework distinguishing continental empires pursuing security through territorial conquest (China, Russia) from maritime powers pursuing prosperity through trade (Britain, US). The lecture examines how geography constrains major powers and shaped global power dynamics, particularly regarding Putin and Xi.

videos.title: Claude Mythos 5 + Fable 5 Are Here And The Numbers Are INSANE
videos.summary: Anthropic released Claude Mythos 5 and Fable 5 (Mythos 5 with guardrails), with Fable 5 included free on Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans through June 22. After that date, Fable 5 access will require usage credits, with plans to restore it as a standard subscription feature when capacity permits.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 plays Factorio
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 autonomously plays Factorio, a factory-building game, demonstrating the ability to strategize and construct an automated factory without human intervention. The demonstration showcases the model's capacity for extended reasoning and planning in a complex simulation environment.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 simulates the solar system and predicts a solar eclipse
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 built a simulation of the solar system from physics first principles to model planetary orbital motion and predict solar eclipses. The model demonstrated the ability to derive complex physical dynamics without explicit training on astronomical data.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 sets a fluid simulation to Beethoven
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 generated code for a fluid simulation synchronized to an EDM remix of Beethoven that it also produced. The model created music without prior exposure to audio.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 designs a 3D-printable model in a Claude-built CAD editor
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 demonstrated designing a 3D-printable model using a browser-based CAD editor that Fable 5 itself created. The editor includes a built-in AI copilot for automated modeling assistance.

videos.title: RAG is dead, right?? — Kuba Rogut, Turbopuffer
videos.summary: Kuba Rogut argues that RAG is not dead but rather misunderstood as vector search alone. RAG encompasses vector search, full text search, glob, regex, and filters used iteratively by agents. He contrasts Cursor's approach of upfront indexing with Claude Code's approach of grepping per session, framing embeddings as cached compute whose value depends on query volume.

videos.title: GPU-accelerated virtual drug screening with cuML and Agent Platform
videos.summary: A Google Cloud livestream demonstrating GPU-accelerated virtual drug screening using cuML and Google Cloud's Agent Platform. The session showed 20x-45x training speedups for molecular binding predictions using cuDF and cuML on GPU hardware, enabling screening at trillion molecule scales without rewriting pandas or scikit-learn code.

videos.title: From Transcription to Live Music: Gemini's Audio Stack — Thor Schaeff, Google DeepMind
videos.summary: Gemini 3 Flash Preview processes audio in a single API call to extract speaker labels, timestamps, emotion tags, language detection with translation, and summaries. It serves as the audio understanding foundation for downstream tasks in Google's audio stack.

videos.title: Stop Prompting Claude. Use Karpathy's Method Instead.
videos.summary: Austin Marchese presents "Karpathy's Method," a three-layer approach attributed to Andrej Karpathy for using Claude more effectively. The method aims to improve development speed and unlock Claude's full potential compared to standard prompting techniques.

videos.title: How To Design a Complete Brand Identity With AI (Best AI Logo Maker)
videos.summary: Design.com is a tool for generating complete brand identities including AI logos, brand kits, and marketing collateral in under 20 minutes. The platform uses plain-English editing and auto-applies brand guidelines across 500,000+ templates including business cards, social posts, and animated logos.

videos.title: Introducing Gemini 3.5 Live Translate
videos.summary: Google DeepMind introduces Gemini 3.5 Live Translate, a speech-to-speech translation model supporting real-time multilingual translation across 70+ languages. The model enables live dubbing of video content, automatic language switching, and natural-sounding output, available to developers via the Gemini API and in products like Google Translate and Google Meet.

videos.title: Kimi Code is good at video reasoning with Kimi K2.6 🎬
videos.summary: Kimi Code is a video reasoning tool built on Kimi K2.6 that accepts reference videos and generates .cube LUT (color lookup table) files based on visual queries about colors, shots, or visual style. Users can drag in video references and request specific visual attributes to generate ready-to-use color grading outputs.

videos.title: How I'd Learn AI From Scratch in 2026 (skip the useless 80%)
videos.summary: Jeff Su presents a prioritized learning strategy for AI in 2026 centered on picking one model to master, building practical context through real projects, and assembling AI systems with tools. The guidance dismisses rote prompting frameworks in favor of deeper model understanding and contextual work.

videos.title: Finally… A NotebookLM Alternative With an API
videos.summary: Open Notebook is a self-hosted, open-source alternative to Google NotebookLM that allows developers to manage documents, code, research, and AI workflows with an API. It supports features like grounded question-answering over custom sources and podcast generation from documents.

videos.title: This is why my AI Agents never guess
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery discusses why their AI agents are designed to avoid guessing or uncertainty in their responses. The video appears to promote a principle of deterministic or reliable AI agent behavior.

videos.title: Building AI Agent Systems and Scaling Challenges in Agentic AI
videos.summary: Sam Anthony discusses challenges in scaling agentic AI systems, highlighting how growth increases cost, latency, and failure risk. Multi-agent design, coordination, and system architecture are presented as critical factors for managing real-world scalability.

videos.title: Code with Claude 2026 | Tokyo
videos.summary: Anthropic held Code with Claude 2026 in Tokyo, a conference featuring announcements about Claude models, the Claude Platform, and Claude Code, with demonstrations from partner organizations Canva, Mizuho, and NRI.

videos.title: Frontier Code (GPT-5.6 VS Mythos): This BENCHMARK is ACTUALLY REAL!
videos.summary: Cognition's FrontierCode is a benchmark designed to measure whether AI-generated code is mergeable and production-ready, using blocker criteria, weighted scores, and maintainer-defined rubrics. It covers 36 flagship open-source repositories across multiple programming languages and reports lower false positive rates than SWE-Bench Pro.

videos.title: Gemma Playground: Robot Duck
videos.summary: Xavier Plantaz demonstrates running Gemma 4 E2B on Raspberry Pi 5 and Jetson Orin Nano using LiteRT for on-device inference. The system uses Parakeet for speech-to-text, Gemma 4 for multimodal inference, and Kokoro for text-to-speech to enable real-time conversational interaction with robotic hardware.

videos.title: New BEST local AI image generator is here!
videos.summary: Ideogram 4 is presented as a local AI image generator offering features like text rendering, bounding box control, and prompt adherence. The video demonstrates its integration with ComfyUI and compares it to other open-source image generation tools.

videos.title: 2026 AI Engineer Vibe Reel
videos.summary: The AI Engineer channel is promoting the World's Fair event in San Francisco scheduled for June 29 to July 2. Tickets and schedule information are available at ai.engineer/wf.

videos.title: Graphify + Obsidian + Claude Code = CHEAT CODE
videos.summary: A tutorial demonstrating how to combine Graphify's repository analysis capabilities with Obsidian's knowledge management system and Claude Code to improve code understanding and generation. The workflow extracts insights from code repositories into a knowledge graph that integrates with Obsidian vaults to enhance Claude's contextual accuracy.

videos.title: ChatGPT Is Getting Its Biggest Upgrade Ever
videos.summary: OpenAI is reportedly planning to transform ChatGPT from a chatbot into a comprehensive AI super app integrating coding, agents, image tools, third-party integrations, and workplace automation. The update includes new security features like Lockdown Mode to protect against prompt injection attacks and represents a shift toward positioning ChatGPT as a central AI layer for work and daily tasks.

videos.title: Working Like a Lawyer with Claude
videos.summary: Mark Pike from Anthropic and Anna Gressel from Freshfields discuss how lawyers use Claude for legal work. They explore how AI is transforming legal practice while emphasizing that final judgment remains with the lawyer.

videos.title: Stop managing infrastructure for AI Agents 🛠️
videos.summary: Google announced managed Agents in the Gemini API that handle infrastructure provisioning and execution. The service runs autonomous agents in a remote Google-hosted sandbox via a single API call, reducing operational overhead for developers.

videos.title: Intro to multi-agent systems with ADK
videos.summary: Google released the Agent Development Kit (ADK), a framework designed to simplify building and testing multi-agent systems. The tutorial demonstrates creating agents with custom tools including real-time date retrieval and Google Search integration, along with a built-in web interface for debugging.

videos.title: Claude Code + Graphify = Insane Agentic OS
videos.summary: Graphify builds a knowledge graph representation of code projects, allowing Claude to understand repository structure without re-reading entire codebases. This reduces token costs, latency, and improves accuracy when working with Claude Code for agentic systems.

videos.title: Road to 5 Million Tokens: Breaking Barriers in Long Context Training — Max Ryabinin, Together AI
videos.summary: Max Ryabinin presents techniques for training LLMs with million-token context windows on limited hardware. The stack combines fully sharded data parallelism, DeepSpeed Ulysses context parallelism, activation checkpointing, CPU offloading, and chunked sequence training. A novel Untied Ulysses variant further reduces activation memory by chunking attention heads and reusing buffers across iterations.

videos.title: Reflecting on a year of Claude Code
videos.summary: Claude Code is an agentic coding tool that runs in the terminal, now widely adopted by developers and organizations. The video reflects on its first year, covering verification practices, auto mode, multi-role adoption, routines for CI and code review, and the shift toward context minimalism.

videos.title: AI YouTube Automation: How to Start a YouTube Channel With AI
videos.summary: A YouTube tutorial demonstrating a complete AI-driven workflow for creating and publishing video content, covering research, scripting, avatar generation, editing, and repurposing. The video walks through 11 stages using multiple tools including ChatGPT, Claude, HeyGen, Descript, and Opus Clip to produce a finished video in approximately 20 minutes.

videos.title: Simulating Percolation in Python: How Do Wildfires & Diseases Spread?
videos.summary: This video tutorial demonstrates how to implement percolation simulations in Python to model the spread of wildfires and diseases. Percolation theory is used to understand how phenomena propagate through networks and systems.

videos.title: Did Apple (Finally) Get AI Right At WWDC?, Anthropic’s Worry, Microsoft vs. OpenAI
videos.summary: Alex Kantrowitz and Ranjan Roy discuss Apple's on-device AI strategy at WWDC, Anthropic's claims about AI self-improvement capabilities, and the emerging competitive dynamics between Microsoft and OpenAI. They explore tensions between AI safety priorities and commercial pressures facing major AI companies.

videos.title: Inside Google Antigravity 2.0: The complete developer guide | The Agent Factory
videos.summary: Google Antigravity 2.0 is an agentic platform that orchestrates specialized sub-agents for software development tasks. It unbundled from a simple IDE into a full ecosystem with a desktop agent manager, CLI, advanced SDK, and custom IDE. The platform enables developers to use voice prompts and skills to coordinate parallel agents for building, localizing, and deploying applications.
